title: MadWest Rocketry × NASA OSDR — Botanical Sounding Rockets: TREES timelapse growth & Reggies' Veggies gene-expression dataset
description: A one-stop educational hub and research compendium for a high-school sounding-rocket space-biology program (2018, Cesaroni K1620 motor) across three launches, with an open Python analysis pipeline. Launch 1: Arabidopsis test — flown seedlings reportedly flowered earlier, and co-flown ectomycorrhizal spores stayed viable after flight. Launch 2 ('Reggies' Veggies'): Arabidopsis qPCR of stress genes (MPK3, TCH3, CBP60g; UBQ10 reference) on flown vs. launch-site and lab controls. Launch 3 ('TREES', a codename — not a species): Populus tremula seedlings ± ectomycorrhizal (ECM) inoculation, flown vs. ground, imaged as a timelapse to test whether the fungal symbiosis aids flight survival; green-canopy image analysis instead showed the mycorrhizal seedlings peaking then dying back. Data formatted toward NASA OSDR conventions. Independent educational project; not affiliated with or endorsed by NASA.
notes: PLACEHOLDER metadata. Before publishing: (1) replace the creators block with real names (format 'Family, Given'), add 'orcid' where available; (2) set the GitHub 'related_identifiers' once the repo URL exists. Note: the ~1 GB of raw imagery under data/raw/ is git-ignored, so a GitHub-release→Zenodo hook will NOT capture it — upload the raw data package to Zenodo directly (see PUBLISHING.md).
